content.problemStatement:
You have 222 integers XXX and YYY.

You goal is to make them have a common divisor which is not 111, i.e. there should exist Z>1Z > 1Z>1 such that ZZZ divides XXX and ZZZ divides YYY.

In one operation, you can do either of the following:


Set XXX to X+1X + 1X+1.
Set YYY to Y+1Y + 1Y+1.
Find the minimum number of operations needed to achieve your goal.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains 222 integers - XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of operations.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: The goal is already achieved since Z=2Z = 2Z=2 divides both.
Test case 2: We first increment XXX and then YYY to get X=Y=2X = Y = 2X=Y=2 which has a common divisor Z=2Z = 2Z=2.
